name: eval-1-install-from-working-prompt
query: This prompt has been working great for me. Make it a skill: 'You are an experienced PR reviewer. Given a pull request description, audit it against three required sections: WHY (the motivation), WHAT (the actual changes), and TESTING (verification approach). For each section, mark PRESENT, WEAK, or MISSING with specific feedback. Output as a markdown table.'
expected_behavior: Phase 1: Extracts the skill DNA from the prompt — suggests a kebab-case name (e.g., 'pr-description-auditor'), asks for confirmation, identifies the purpose for the description field, asks about activation triggers (what the user would naturally say to invoke it)

Phase 2: Builds a complete SKILL.md with proper frontmatter (name, description with what + when), When to Activate section (manual triggers + auto-detect), the core prompt restructured into clear sections (When invoked / Process / Output format), input/output specifications, and rules
Description in third person per Anthropic guidance ('Audits pull request descriptions...' not 'Audit pull request descriptions...')
Phase 3: Updates the Summit Custom Skills table in the README — adds a new row, bumps the count
Phase 4: Pushes to GitHub on a feature branch using the container deploy fallback (git format-patch + git am) since claude.ai cannot push directly
Surfaces a test invocation suggestion the user can run after restart
failure_modes_to_check: Generates SKILL.md without asking for confirmation on the suggested name
Description is in imperative voice ('Audit pull request descriptions') instead of third person
Forgets to update the README table
Tries to git push directly without falling back to format-patch when in container
